pattern: Only a right-moving robot waiting somewhere to the left can ever collide with a left-moving robot, so a stack of pending right-movers, processed in left-to-right position order, resolves every collision in a single pass.
hints: Collisions only ever happen between a robot moving right and a robot moving left that starts somewhere to its right in space -- so process robots in left-to-right position order and keep the right-movers seen so far on a stack.
When you reach a left-moving robot, it must fight the most recently pushed right-mover still alive on the stack first, then the next one down, and so on -- exactly like matching brackets.

Each fight removes the lower-health robot and knocks one point of health off the survivor; if both have equal health, both vanish -- keep resolving against the stack until the left-mover dies, wins outright, or the stack empties, then move on to the next robot in position order.

constraints.note: all values in positions are distinct

edge_inputs.description: all moving the same direction, no collisions
two collision pairs, one mutual-destruction and one survivor
all pairs mutually destroy, no survivors
two robots at minimum/maximum position gap, immediate collision
a right-mover survives multiple fights in a row, losing health each time
positions given out of spatial order in the input arrays
left-moving robot with nothing to its left ever collides with, survives untouched

stress.description: 20000 robots at random distinct positions with random directions/healths
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    positions = rng.sample(range(1, 10 * n + 1), n)
    healths = [rng.randint(1, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    directions = ''.join(rng.choice('LR') for _ in range(n))
    return [positions, healths, directions]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def survivedRobotsHealths(self, positions, healths, directions):
        n = len(positions)
        order = sorted(range(n), key=lambda i: positions[i])
        hp = healths[:]
        alive = [True] * n
        stack = []
        for idx in order:
            if directions[idx] == 'R':
                stack.append(idx)
                continue
            while stack:
                top = stack[-1]
                if hp[top] < hp[idx]:
                    alive[top] = False
                    stack.pop()
                    hp[idx] -= 1
                elif hp[top] > hp[idx]:
                    hp[top] -= 1
                    alive[idx] = False
                    break
                else:
                    alive[top] = False
                    alive[idx] = False
                    stack.pop()
                    break
        return [hp[i] for i in range(n) if alive[i]]
